mnemonics.title: MIND Model
mnemonics.content:
M - Mental models represent user thinking
I - Information architecture organizes content
N - Navigation guides user journeys
D - Design aligns with user expectations

acronyms.usage: The overall experience a user has when interacting with a product
The structural organization of content in a product
Design approach that prioritizes user needs and preferences
The simplest version of a product that can be released for testing

visualTips.visualization: Picture an iceberg - above water is what users see (interface), below is their mental model (assumptions, expectations)
Think of a comic strip - each panel is a screen, arrows show user actions, facial expressions show emotions
Two overlapping circles - the overlap is the 'sweet spot' where design matches user expectations

analogies.analogy: Like learning to drive different cars - your mental model of 'steering wheel moves car' transfers, but you still adapt to each car's specific controls
Like a movie storyboard showing scenes in sequence before filming begins
Like an architect's blueprint - shows structure and layout without decorative details
